concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: The Table of Contents on page 2 of the source document lists items from 1.0 down to 6.0 REFERENCES. However, the output JSON for page 2 truncates abruptly after item 3.1 ('Project Information and Report Organization'). Following a garbled string of OCR artifacts (repeating zeros), all remaining TOC entries (sections 3.1.1 through 6.0) are completely missing from the output.

I am reviewing links in the document. On page 26 of the PDF (page 26 of the output JSON), there is a link 'Risk Evaluation Resources | NC DEQ'. In the PDF, this is a clickable hyperlink leading to a URL. However, in the JSON output, this is plain text under '3.5 Contaminant Migration Worksheets' as 'Risk Evaluation Resources | NC DEQ' without any link injection. Wait, S7 is about *broken, missing, or fabricated* links (missing means a link is clearly visible/clickable in PDF but NOT clickable in
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
Page 5: "Technical Guidance for Risk-Based Environmental Remediation of Sites"
Page 26: "Risk Evaluation Resources | NC DEQ"
Page 31: "Technical Guidance for Risk-Based Environmental Remediation of Sites"

concerns.analysis: On page 8, the source PDF contains a large, meaningful diagram titled 'Example site with multiple Exposure Units' that illustrates a factory, houses, soil and groundwater contamination, and a potential water supply well. In the HTML output, this composite diagram is missing. Instead, only fragmented parts of it (small icons of the factory and houses) were extracted as separate images, losing the visual context and structural information of the diagram.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: In the source PDF, page 8 contains a large block diagram titled 'Example site with multiple Exposure Units' showing the relationship between a factory, soil/groundwater contamination, and residences.
concerns.output_quote: In the HTML, the main diagram is missing, replaced only by fragmented icons: ``.
concerns.location: Page 8, under the heading '2.4 Exposure Units'

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (1 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=1. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (69): Risk Calculator User Guide; Risk Calculator User Guide; February 2024
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=69, pages_affected=31/33. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Footnote(s) converted to bulleted list: 1 unordered list(s) after table(s) appear to be footnotes
concerns.analysis: footnote_as_list_count=1. Examples: 1 item(s): "Chemical with a Henry's Law constant less than or ". Table footnotes (often marked with *, †, ‡) were converted to <ul><li> elements instead of paragraphs. This changes the semantic meaning — screen readers announce them as list items rather than explanatory notes.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "ASTM"
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=1. Tables use <th> for data values (misrepresenting data as headers) or have a data row mismarked as a header row. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 34 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (31 truncated mid-sentence, 3 generic placeholder(s)) — e.g. p1: "...y. The design features a blue outline map of North"; p6: "...nmental contaminants from initial release to human"; p8: "... and a black house icon on the right, separated by"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=34 (truncated=31, placeholder=3, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

The source PDF uses underline formatting in several places to denote emphasis. For example, on PDF page 25 (document page 21), the word 'all' is explicitly underlined ('To ensure that all site risks...'). On PDF page 20 (document page 16), the phrase 'each detected contaminant' is underlined. The output json strips this underline completely, losing the structural emphasis.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: To ensure that all site risks are within the allowable limits
concerns.output_quote: To ensure that all site risks are within the allowable limits

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=1. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.
